Subject: Caught! - Florida Man

Subject: Caught! - Trains, Planes and Flying Fists
Description: By land, air, rail or sea -- with so many ways to travel, one'd think it would be a breeze; but leave it to fellow travelers to bring one to one's knees, causing unexpected turmoil along the way.

Subject: Caught! - Weekend Warriors
Description: After a seemingly endless work week, millions of people blow off steam on the weekend -- and that's when things get wild; from day drinking to house parties and late-night dinners, the big fight weekend kicks off the chaos.

Subject: Caught! - Wild on the Water
Description: The open seas teem with mischief, patrolled by the U.S. Coast Guard and Marine Law Enforcement to ensure safety; officers leap to capture runaway boats, rescue families from dangerous waters, save ice anglers, and outsmart drug traffickers.

Subject: Caught! - Indecent and Exposed
Description: Disorderly conduct ranges from public streaking to getting personal in public spaces; people can't seem to keep their passion private; an argument at a gas station leads one woman to an epic flip-out.

Subject: Caught! - Service Without a Smile
Description: Serving others is a noble cause, unless it's in the service industry; between bad customers and broken machines, it can be a total nightmare.

Description: Many people between the ages of 25 and 45 who get caught in chainsaw mishaps, beehive blunders and more, learn why it's best to leave it to the pros.

Description: Kardea Brown is keeping Thanksgiving intimate with a special meal for Ma and Grandma; Grandma always brings the turkey, so Kardea is taking on the sides, with candied yams, oyster stuffing and cranberry custard pie for dessert.

Subject: Guy's Ranch Kitchen - Thanksgiving Melting Pot
Description: Guy Fieri invites chefs to share some Turkey Day dishes that speak to their personal heritage; G. Garvin brings Georgia flavor north of the Mason-Dixon Line; Eric Greenspan makes his bubbie's challah for a spicy turkey pastilla.

Subject: Guy's Ranch Kitchen - Thanksgiving Simplified
Description: Thanksgiving simplified with some of Guy's favorite chefs; Antonia Lofaso crisps cauliflower with lemony aioli; Marc Murphy makes kabocha ravioli; Michael Voltaggio saves the best leftovers for last with some pumpkin pie egg rolls.

Subject: Guy's Ranch Kitchen - International Thanksgiving
Description: Guy Fieri gives thanks with international flair; Chef Nyesha Arrington cooks chorizo clam toast; Chef Nate Appleman presents turkey porchetta; Chef Shota Nakajima gives gochujang miso glazed carrots; Chef Jet Tila bakes a chile mango cake.

Subject: Alex vs. America - Alex vs. Winners

Subject: Alex vs. America - Alex vs Frozen Desserts
Description: Eric Adjepong challenges three cool treat chefs to take on Alex; Duff Goldman and Natasha Case judge as the chefs try to keep their cool to beat Alex.

Subject: Chopped - Grand Champs' Holiday Hurrah
Description: Four Grand Champions are headed home to the Chopped kitchen for the holidays; the chefs are challenged with some extremely tough baskets, beginning with a kooky candy cane in the appetizer round.

Subject: Chopped - Trash Into Treasure
Description: The chefs turn edible trash into culinary treasures in a zero-waste challenge; they have big plans for shrimp shells, leftover meals, and vegetable tops before the two finalists make wise use of old milk before time expires in the dessert round..

Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - Loaded, Stuffed and Fried

Description: Easy Company enters Germany and discovers an abandoned Nazi concentration camp occupied by emaciated prisoners, just as the local citizens learn that Hitler is dead.

Subject: Band of Brothers - Points
Description: After capturing Eagle's Nest, Hitler's mountaintop fortress, the men of Easy Company face deployment to the Pacific.

Subject: Law & Order - Narcosis
Description: The detectives' investigation into the strangulation of an Asian-American stripper leads them to corrupt officers.

Subject: Law & Order - High & Low
Description: The detectives struggle to determine the motive of a club owner who hired two men to kill a stripper.

Subject: Law & Order - Stiff
Description: The detectives suspect a comatose woman's husband tried either to commit a mercy killing or to kill her for her money.

Subject: Law & Order - Vaya con Dios
Description: McCoy seeks to prosecute a high-level foreign dignitary for the torture and murder of a New York student 27 years earlier.

Subject: Hidden Habitats - Svalbard
Description: In the archipelago of Svalbard in the Arctic Circle, the ecosystem is driven by the producers at the bottom of the food chain.

Subject: Hidden Habitats - Yellowstone
Description: Wolves are released into Yellowstone National Park after a 70-year absence, setting off a series of events within the food chain.

Subject: Chasing the Rains - Praying for Rain
Description: Anastasia's herd splits to protect two precious elephant calves; Kuleta retakes territory for her remaining cheetah cubs; Phoenix searches for a new mate; the skies open up and rain begins to heal the parched land.

Subject: Band of Brothers - Bastogne
Description: Easy Company spends Christmas in the trenches while trying to hold the line in the forest outside Bastogne, Belgium.

Subject: Band of Brothers - The Breaking Point
Description: After taking Bastogne, Belgium, the company moves on to the town of Foy, where many lives are lost as a result of Lt. Dike's incompetence.

Subject: Band of Brothers - The Last Patrol
Description: Lt. Jones leads a patrol across a river to take enemy prisoners, but the loss of a paratrooper leads Winters to refuse to send another patrol the next night.

Subject: The Rainmaker (1997)
Description: A Memphis law school graduate (Matt Damon) aids a battered wife (Claire Danes) and fights a corporate lawyer (Jon Voight) for insurance benefits for a dying man.

Subject: Kindergarten Cop (1990)
Description: An undercover officer (Arnold Schwarzenegger) teaches a rowdy class of peewees, to identify one whose mother must be found.

Subject: U.S. Marshals (1998)
Description: A chief deputy (Tommy Lee Jones) and his team pursue an escaped fugitive (Wesley Snipes) accused of killing two government agents.

Subject: A Few Good Men (1992)
Description: Navy lawyers (Tom Cruise, Demi Moore) defend two Marines accused of killing a private at the naval station at Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.

Subject: Law & Order - Endurance
Description: A boy with severe disabilities dies from smoke inhalation in a suspicious fire in an apartment building.

Subject: Law & Order - Turnstile Justice
Description: A woman is found dead of a head wound with no clues to her identity other than her mass-transit pass.

Subject: Law & Order - Dissonance
Description: Briscoe and Green investigate the murder of a young violinist whose fellow musicians resented her rapid rise to fame.

Subject: Law & Order - Standoff
Description: The stabbing death of a gang leader in a prison scuffle has Briscoe and Green looking at both inmates and corrections officers as possible suspects.

Subject: Law & Order - Return
Description: A murder suspect flees to Israel and may be protected from extradition by Israel's Law of Return.

Subject: Law & Order - Burn Baby Burn
Description: A former Black Panther accused of murdering a Caucasian police officer questions Detective Green's integrity while a politically charged trial ensues.

Subject: Law & Order - Amends
Description: The detectives uncover new evidence implicating a wealthy scion in the murder of a teenage girl 20 years earlier.

Subject: The Three Stooges - A Pain in the Pullman
Description: The boys wreak havoc on a train.

Subject: Bones - Spaceman in a Crater
Description: When Brennan and Booth investigate a body that was found in a crater, evidence suggests that it had been in outer space.

Subject: Bones - The Glowing Bones in the Old Stone House
Description: Potentially radioactive remains emit a green glow at the crime scene.

Subject: Bones - Stargazer in a Puddle
Description: Brennan and Booth investigate a small skeleton assumed to be that of a 10-year-old girl; Brennan's father (Ryan O'Neal) reappears in her life to deliver information from her past.

Subject: Bones - The Widow's Son in the Windshield
Description: A skull smashes the windshield of a car driven by a group of teenagers on the freeway; a trail of suspects leads Brennan and Booth to a strange cannibalistic society; Cam suggests that Angela hire a private investigator to find her ex-husband.

Subject: Bones - The Soccer Mom in the Mini-Van
Description: Brennan and Booth investigate when a soccer mom is blown up in her minivan; the investigation uncovers that the woman was a member of the National Liberation Army.

Subject: U.S. Marshals (1998)
Description: A chief deputy (Tommy Lee Jones) and his team pursue an escaped fugitive (Wesley Snipes) accused of killing two government agents.

Subject: A Few Good Men (1992)
Description: Navy lawyers (Tom Cruise, Demi Moore) defend two Marines accused of killing a private at the naval station at Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.

Subject: Gran Torino (2008)
Description: An unlikely friendship forms between a bigoted war veteran (Clint Eastwood) and an Asian boy (Bee Vang) who tried to steal the man's treasured automobile.

Subject: Moneyball (2011)
Description: Faced with a tight budget, a baseball manager and his partner recruit bargain-bin players that scouts have labeled as flawed, but have game-winning potential.

Subject: Law & Order - Thin Ice
Description: The beating death of a youth hockey coach leads Briscoe and Green to suspect a player with an inclination for rough play.

Subject: Law & Order - Hubris
Description: A charming real-estate broker accused of murder defends himself and manages to get key evidence thrown out of the trial.

Subject: Law & Order - Whose Monkey Is It Anyway
Description: Several animal rights activists are under suspicion when an AIDS researcher performing tests on primates is murdered in the lab.

Subject: Law & Order - Sunday in the Park With Jorge
Description: Briscoe and Green must determine whether a woman's death was related to a rash of attacks by a crazed mob in Central Park.

Subject: Law & Order - Teenage Wasteland
Description: McCoy seeks the death penalty in the case of a teenager accused of murdering a restaurant owner during an ill-fated delivery.

Subject: Law & Order - Phobia
Description: A gay man is murdered and his adopted son taken in a case that first looks like a kidnapping until the facts uncover the possibility of a hate crime.

Subject: Law & Order - A Losing Season
Description: A pro basketball player is a suspect in a shooting that killed a pregnant banker and left her fetus clinging to life.

Subject: Hidden Habitats - Great Barrier Reef
Description: The Great Barrier Reef exists in nutrient-poor waters, in spite of its immense size.

Subject: Hidden Habitats - Them & Us
Description: The connections between humans and insects.

Subject: Law & Order - Swept Away: A Very Special Episode
Description: When a murder happens on a reality television program, investigators try to identify the real culprits.

Subject: Law & Order - Bronx Cheer
Description: While investigating a murder, Briscoe and Green discover their prime suspect may be guilty of a homicide another man has been convicted of.

Subject: Gridiron Gang (2006)
Description: A counselor (The Rock) at a juvenile detention facility turns a group of young criminals into a football team to teach them self-respect and responsibility.

Subject: Gran Torino (2008)
Description: An unlikely friendship forms between a bigoted war veteran (Clint Eastwood) and an Asian boy (Bee Vang) who tried to steal the man's treasured automobile.

Subject: Moneyball (2011)
Description: Faced with a tight budget, a baseball manager and his partner recruit bargain-bin players that scouts have labeled as flawed, but have game-winning potential.

Subject: Miracle (2004)
Description: Coach Herb Brooks (Kurt Russell) leads the U.S. Olympic hockey team to victory over the Soviet team in 1980.

Subject: Rudy (1993)
Description: With heart and determination an Illinois youth (Sean Astin) tackles shortcomings to play Notre Dame football.

Subject: Criminal Minds - Nanny Dearest
Description: The BAU team heads to Los Angeles in search of a suspect who kidnaps nannies and the children they care for.

Subject: Criminal Minds - No. 6
Description: The team tracks a suspect targeting people in Detroit; Blake receives a life-changing proposition from her husband (D.W. Moffett).

Subject: Criminal Minds - Brothers Hotchner
Description: The team investigates a series of deaths in Manhattan that seem to be related to ecstasy overdoses; Hotch makes a discovery about his estranged brother.

Subject: Criminal Minds - The Replicator
Description: When the Replicator returns, a member of the BAU team is targeted.

Subject: Criminal Minds - The Inspiration
Description: The team tracks an increasingly devolving killer to Arizona; the team suspects Hotch is in the running to be the unit chief.

Subject: Criminal Minds - The Inspired
Description: As the hunt for a ritualistic killer continues, a bizarre familial connection could help the team zero in on the suspect.

Subject: Criminal Minds - Final Shot
Description: The BAU team must determine if the anniversary of the Kennedy assassination is a motivating factor when a sniper strikes Dallas.

Subject: The Three Stooges - Rhythm & Weep
Description: An opportune offer saves the suicidal Stooges.

Subject: NCIS - Extreme Prejudice
Description: Following the bombing of NCIS headquarters, the team engages in a cross-agency manhunt for Harper Dearing.

Subject: NCIS - Recovery
Description: The team investigates when the body of an NCIS facilities manager is found four months after she went missing; as the team faces mandatory psych evaluations, Gibbs is concerned about Abby's recurring nightmares.

Subject: NCIS - Phoenix
Description: Ducky orders the exhumation of a body from a 12-year-old case; the team investigates a Marine's death.

Subject: NCIS - Lost at Sea
Description: When a helicopter crew's training mission ends in a disastrous crash, the NCIS team must figure out what happened and find the missing pilot; Ziva challenges McGee and Tony to a dare.

Subject: NCIS - The Namesake
Description: When a petty officer is shot in a billionaire's car, the investigation leads the team to Gibbs' namesake.

Subject: NCIS - Shell Shock, Part I
Description: When a Navy lieutenant is killed in a brutal attack shortly after returning home from the Middle East, the team questions the victim's friend, a Marine captain with post-traumatic stress disorder.

Subject: Dr. No (1962)
Description: Agent 007 (Sean Connery) foils a SPECTRE madman (Joseph Wiseman) out to divert rockets from Cape Canaveral.

Subject: From Russia With Love (1963)
Description: Agent 007 arrives in Istanbul, bringing with him a Russian beauty who is an unwitting pawn in SPECTRE's plot to kill him.

Subject: Goldfinger (1964)
Description: Agent 007 (Sean Connery) drives an Aston Martin, runs into Oddjob and fights Goldfinger's (Gert Frobe) scheme to rob Fort Knox.

Subject: Skyfall (2012)
Description: When M's (Judi Dench) past comes back to haunt her, James Bond (Daniel Craig) must track down and destroy the threat to her and MI6, no matter how personal the cost.

Subject: The Graham Norton Show
Description: Hugh Jackman, Kate Hudson, Ben Stiller, Da'Vine Joy Randolph and singer-songwriter Sombr performs his single 12 to 12.

Subject: Goldfinger (1964)
Description: Agent 007 (Sean Connery) drives an Aston Martin, runs into Oddjob and fights Goldfinger's (Gert Frobe) scheme to rob Fort Knox.

Subject: Skyfall (2012)
Description: When M's (Judi Dench) past comes back to haunt her, James Bond (Daniel Craig) must track down and destroy the threat to her and MI6, no matter how personal the cost.

Subject: The Three Stooges - Three Little Pirates
Description: The marooned trio is condemned.

Subject: Bones - The Priest in the Churchyard
Description: When a water main bursts at a cemetery, Brennan must identify and help reinter the remains that litter the grounds.

Subject: Bones - The Boneless Bride in the River
Description: Booth interrupts Brennan's vacation with her boyfriend to get help with the case of a woman whose boneless body was found in a river.

Subject: Bones - Bodies in the Book
Description: During a murder investigation at the marina, Booth notices similarities between the case and a murder in Brennan's latest book.

Subject: Bones - Man in the Mansion
Description: The Bancroft family calls on Brennan and Booth to investigate the stabbing death of Terence Bancroft; Agent Sullivan invites Brennan to his basketball game.

Subject: Bones - The Girl in the Gator
Description: After Booth's gun is taken away because he maliciously shot a clown ornament on an ice-cream truck, Brennan must go to Florida alone to investigate the death of a college student whose body is found inside an alligator.

Subject: College Football - Georgia Tech at Boston College
Description: The No. 16 Yellow Jackets (8-1, 5-1) refocus on the ACC title game as they play the Eagles (1-9, 0-6). Entering Week 12, Georgia Tech sat in a four-game tie for first with Virginia, Pittsburgh and SMU after a 48-36 upset loss at NC State on Nov. 1.

Subject: ACC Huddle
Description: Host Taylor Tannebaum and analysts Jimbo Fisher, Eddie Royal and Eric Mac Lain go on the road each week to preview the slate of ACC football games. Live from campus of the ACC's marquee game of the week.

Subject: College Football - Delaware at Wake Forest
Description: Delaware (5-5) plays Wake Forest (7-3) from Winston-Salem, N.C. The Demon Deacons beat the Blue Hens 38-21 in the last meeting in 2016. Entering Week 13, Delaware quarterback Nick Minicucci sat second in the nation with 3,063 passing yards.

Subject: College Football - Duke at North Carolina
Description: The Blue Devils traverse Tobacco Road to play the Tar Heels for the Victory Bell. In 2024, Duke erased a 20-0 second-half deficit to beat North Carolina 21-20 and end a five-game series skid. UNC must win to keep its hopes of bowl eligibility alive.

Subject: College Football - California at Stanford
Description: The Cardinal host the Golden Bears. Stanford leads the all-time series 65-51-11, but California has won four straight meetings after rallying to keep the streak alive in 2024. Cal stunned then-No. 15 Louisville in overtime prior to its bye Nov. 8.

Description: The Mustangs (7-3, 5-1 ACC) march into this date with the Cardinals (7-3, 4-3). SMU won five of its past six games prior to its bye. Louisville's College Football Playoff hopes have taken a hit after losses to unranked California and Clemson.

Subject: College Football - Florida State at NC State
Description: The Wolfpack (5-5, 2-4) and Seminoles (5-5, 2-5) play for bowl eligibility in this ACC contest. NC State has dropped three of four to put it at risk of missing the postseason for the first time in four years. The Wolfpack won the last three meetings.

Subject: College Football - Pittsburgh at Georgia Tech
Description: The Panthers line up against the No. 16 Yellow Jackets. Georgia Tech bounced back from its first loss of the season by outlasting Boston College, 36-34 on Nov. 15. Pittsburgh had a five-game win streak snapped in a loss to then-No. 9 Notre Dame.

Subject: College Football - NC State at Miami
Description: The Wolfpack set out to play spoiler once more by visiting the No. 15 Hurricanes. NC State upset then-No. 8 Georgia Tech 48-36 prior to its bye Nov. 1. Freshman Jayden Scott (NCSU) scored and set a new career high with 196 rushing yards in the win.

Subject: College Basketball - Presbyterian at UCLA
Description: The Blue Hose challenge the No. 19 Bruins. Presbyterian is 1-3 on its current five-game road trip, and 3-4 overall this season. UCLA moved to 4-0 at Pauley Pavilion in 2025-26, and 4-1 overall, with a 79-48 beatdown of Sacramento State on Nov. 18.

Subject: College Football - Jacksonville State at Florida International

Subject: Swiss Stone Tossing

Subject: The Goodyear Blimp Sky Race of the Century

Subject: Microsoft Excel World Championship 2024 Finals

Subject: School of Golf - Chapter 1: Solid Start
Description: Martin Hall and Blair O'Neal are back on the range teaching how to get off to a solid start this golf season; ball striking tips used by greats; Hall reveals how two empty sleeves of golf balls can improve ball striking.

Subject: School of Golf - Chapter 2: Better Bunker Play
Description: Martin Hall and Blair O'Neal teach a variety of shots from the sand, including how to hit green-side bunker shots. Martin also shares fairway bunker tips he learned from Rory McIlroy, and Tom Watson shares his advice for dealing with plugged lies.

Subject: School of Golf - Chapter 3: Total Driving
Description: Martin Hall and Blair O'Neal show which setup, swing, and equipment adjustments translate into driving the ball longer and straighter. Martin shares driving tips from Dustin Johnson and discusses how to find the proper driver shaft length.

Subject: School of Golf - Chapter 4: Chip Shot Mastery
Description: Martin Hall and Blair O'Neal share chipping tips from 10 Masters champions including Tiger Woods, Gary Player, Phil Mickelson, and Nick Faldo; how to scramble from just off the green, hit long running chips, and make crisp contact from tight lies.

Subject: School of Golf - Chapter 5: Beyond the Ball
Description: The importance of the follow through with drills on improving the swing post-impact; Ben Hogan's picture frame finish stops the hook; Henrik Stenson stops the slice; how arm wrestling can improve the golf swing.

Subject: School of Golf - Chapter 6: Slice and Hook Cures
Description: Martin Hall and Blair O'Neal show which setup, swing, and equipment adjustments translate into stopping slices and hooks. Martin shares some of his favorite drills on finding the proper grip, maintaining balance, and swinging on the proper plane.

Subject: School of Golf - Chapter 9: Putting Class
Description: Martin Hall explains how to become a better putter ASAP; those four letters standing for aim, stability, arc, and pace. Martin shares drills for each letter from putting gurus including Dave Stockton, Phil Kenyon, and the late Frank Thomas.

Description: From Sea Island Golf Club - Seaside Course in Sea Island, Ga.

Subject: School of Golf - Chapter 21: Positive Impact
Description: Martin Hall and Blair O'Neal focus on impact position. Martin analyzes the swing of 2021 Open Champion Collin Morikawa, shares a drill used by legendary ball striker Johnny Miller, and reveals a tip on making solid contact from an unlikely source.

Subject: School of Golf - Chapter 22: Right Or Rubbish
Description: Martin Hall and Blair O'Neal analyze the world's best players to bust popular myths about the golf swing. Learn what ``cover it`` means to Rory McIlroy, a tip to dial in wedge shots like Jordan Spieth.

Subject: College Football - Kentucky at Vanderbilt
Description: The No. 14 Commodores (8-2, 4-2 SEC) attempt to equal a school record as they host the Wildcats (5-5, 2-5). Vanderbilt's most victories in a season is nine, accomplished in 2013, 2012, 1915 and 1904. The Commodores won two of the last three meetings.

Subject: College Football - Coastal Carolina at South Carolina
Description: The Chanticleers line up against the Gamecocks in a non-conference outing. Coastal Carolina had a four-game win streak snapped in its last match, a 45-40 loss at Georgia Southern on Nov. 15. South Carolina beat Coastal Carolina 49-15 in 2018.

Subject: College Football - Tennessee at Florida
Description: The No. 20 Volunteers (7-3, 3-3 SEC) visit the Gators (3-7, 2-5). The rivalry series, typically referred to as the Third Sunday in September, was played in October in 2024. Tennessee cut into Florida's 32-22 all-time lead with a 23-17 overtime win.
